title: Savoury Crêpes with Mushroom and Cheese

subs.name: half maida and half wheat flour (atta) — gives a slightly more rustic, wholesome crêpe
subs.notes: may need a little extra milk

ingredients.name: cold water
visual_checks: thins the batter to the right consistency
ingredients.notes: thins the batter to the right consistency

ingredients.name: butter
visual_checks: 1 tbsp in batter, 1 tbsp for cooking
ingredients.notes: 1 tbsp in batter, 1 tbsp for cooking

subs.name: capsicum, blanched broccoli, or corn kernels — any firm vegetable that holds up to sautéing
subs.notes: cook out the moisture fully before filling

subs.name: fenugreek leaves (methi) — wash well to remove bitterness; adds a pleasant depth
Amul processed cheese slices (1 per crêpe) — melt quickly and evenly
subs.notes: cheddar blocks also work well grated

steps.text: Whisk together the maida, egg, milk, cold water, a pinch of salt and 1 tablespoon of the melted butter in a bowl until perfectly smooth — no lumps. The batter should be thin, about the consistency of single cream. Cover and rest for 30 minutes at room temperature.

While the batter rests, melt the remaining butter in a frying pan over medium heat. Add the onion and cook 3 minutes until softened, then add the garlic and cook 1 minute more. Add the mushrooms and cook, stirring occasionally, for 5–6 minutes until their moisture has evaporated and they begin to brown.

Add the rinsed spinach to the mushrooms and stir until it wilts, about 2 minutes. Strip the leaves from the thyme sprigs if using and stir them in. Season the filling generously with salt and pepper and keep warm off the heat.

Heat a 22 cm non-stick pan or tawa over medium heat. Brush lightly with a little butter using a folded cloth. Pour in a small ladle of batter (about 60 ml) and swirl the pan immediately to form a thin, even round. Cook for about 1 minute until the edges lift and the underside is pale gold.

Flip the crêpe and cook the second side for 30 seconds. Place 2–3 tablespoons of the mushroom filling across one half of the crêpe and fold the crêpe in half over the filling. Scatter a tablespoon of grated cheese on top, cover the pan with a lid for 1 minute so the cheese melts, then slide onto a plate.
Repeat with the remaining batter and filling to make 8–10 crêpes in total. Serve immediately — savoury crêpes are best straight from the pan and do not hold well.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
critical_checks: Use pasteurised eggs for preparations served raw or lightly cooked, especially for higher-risk people.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: mushroom → capsicum, blanched broccoli, or corn kernels — any firm vegetable that holds up to sautéing (cook out the moisture fully before filling); cheese → Amul processed cheese slices (1 per crêpe) — melt quickly and evenly (cheddar blocks also work well grated); spinach → fenugreek leaves (methi) — wash well to remove bitterness; adds a pleasant depth (wilt briefly before using); maida → half maida and half wheat flour (atta) — gives a slightly more rustic, wholesome crêpe (may need a little extra milk)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: A flat non-stick dosa tawa is ideal and makes the process feel familiar. Amul cheddar (block, grated) melts beautifully and is widely available. The mushroom filling can be enhanced with a pinch of garam masala or black pepper for a desi twist. Paneer can replace the cheese for a protein-rich alternative.
